metadata.description: A virtual compute instance with CPU, memory, a guest OS, and optional attached disks and network interfaces.

instance_size.description: Provider-neutral size class (small|medium|large, ordered/comparable — common-elements §2.2, ADR-014). Size by class IN PLACE OF vcpu+memory; the provider maps it to concrete resources at naturalization (ADR-023). oneOf enforces sizing by class XOR raw (exactly one).

count.description: Total virtual CPUs. The provider naturalizes this to its native cpu model (k8s/kubevirt domain cpu, vSphere sockets*cores, EC2 vCPUs) at realization (ADR-023).

sockets.description: Optional topology hint: sockets. Provider-support-dependent; ignored where not applicable.

cores_per_socket.description: Optional topology hint: cores per socket. Provider-support-dependent.

hugepages.description: Optional huge-page backing (provider-support-dependent; validated against provider capability).

guest_os.description: Guest operating system. Portable form is a reference to an os_image reference-data vocabulary (ADR-012; layering §3.7) the target provider advertises via capability discovery (ADR-004); policy validates membership at request. Inline is the portability_breaking escape.

type.description: Inline OS identifier (e.g. rhel-9). portability_breaking: a value with no os_image vocabulary entry — a review finding.

storage.description: Storage REQUIREMENTS for this disk — the portable, capability-based intent the provider MATCHES to a native backing (a k8s StorageClass, a vSphere datastore, an oVirt storage domain, a ZFS/LVM pool, a cloud volume type). Requirements-only by design: a named provider class is vendor-native and does not port across bare-metal / VM / k8s, so the chosen class is the provider's REALIZED fact (recorded in outputs), never portable intent (ADR-035). Fields mirror the capabilities a provider advertises (Platform.StorageClass.capabilities), so request and advertisement speak one language; the provider selects a class whose advertised capabilities satisfy these minima.

boot_order.description: Optional boot order (0 = first). Absent = provider default.

placement.description: Placement INTENT expressed as a SELECTION of an existing Facility.Location (populated by a platform data layer and/or a Facility.Location provider), plus affinity relative to other existing resources. Policy governs which locations a given consumer may select; the VM only references one — it does not define locations itself (ADR-009 §4: guidance, not a gate).

location_ref.description: handle/uuid of an existing Facility.Location the VM is placed in — chosen from the eligible set (platform-layer + provider-advertised, policy-filtered). Also carried as a references->Facility.Location relationship.

zone.description: Availability/sovereignty zone constraint, selected from the zones the provider advertises for the chosen location; resolved against that location.

affinity.description: Co-location / separation intent relative to OTHER named resources (not nodes). Each target is resolved at reserve, so a pre-known UUID is not required.

target.description: Handle OR label-selector of an existing resource to co-locate with / avoid, resolved at reserve (ADR-025/AEP-124) - you do not need its UUID in advance.

selector.description: Alternative to a handle: match target(s) by labels (key/value).

networks.description: Network attachment INTENT — each entry SELECTS an existing Network.VirtualNetwork (platform-layer and/or provider-advertised) the NIC attaches to; the responsible network provider + policy decide eligibility. The VM references existing networks, it does not define network segments itself.

network_ref.description: handle/uuid of an existing Network.VirtualNetwork the NIC attaches to (selected from the eligible set). Carried as a references->Network.VirtualNetwork relationship.

ip_address_ref.description: Optional handle/uuid of an existing Network.IPAddress to bind to this NIC (an ADR-025 resource reference, like network_ref). The referenced record carries HOW the address was selected and WHAT it is — its `allocation` origin (static | dhcp | link-layer | random) and `address` CIDR, the RFC 8344 / NMstate convergent form (ADR-023); a static reservation is simply `allocation: static`. ABSENT = the provider allocates an address on `target_segment` at reserve (ADR-009 §3 fulfillment). Host L3 config (routes, DNS, bond/bridge/VLAN) lives in Network.ConnectionProfile (NMstate), not restated here. Carried as a references->Network.IPAddress relationship.

run_state.description: Desired guest RUN state the provider drives to (e.g. KubeVirt runStrategy). Distinct from the four-state lifecycle_state; snapshot/restore are separate operations, not run states.

ip_address.description: Primary IP address assigned once Realized.

target_segment.description: Realize-time placement fact (ADR-011 two-phase reserve): a reference to the Network.VLAN segment the reserved/realized placement can reach (adopt-not-absorb — the segment id/encapsulation live on the Network.VLAN). This is the declarable binding source for a fulfillment:provider dependency whose criteria are simply the target segment — e.g. a Network.IPAddress allocated on the reachable segment (ADR-009 §3 worked example: bind target_segment -> the IP request's segment_ref). It is a shared-reference fact, NOT the vNIC/MAC/host-networking (those are the VM provider's own internal facts, never cross-provider criteria).

run_state.description: Observed guest run state reported by the provider (running/stopped/...), the realized counterpart of spec.run_state.

storage_backing.description: Realized: the native storage backing the provider selected per disk to satisfy spec.disks[].storage (e.g. a k8s StorageClass name, a datastore, a ZFS dataset). Provider-reported, vendor-specific — a realized fact, not portable intent.

relationships.description: May consume zero or more pre-allocated Network.IPAddress addresses (optional dependency).

relationships.name: placement
relationships.description: Placed in an existing Facility.Location (selected, policy-governed) — see spec.placement.location_ref.

relationships.name: network_attachment
relationships.description: Attaches to existing Network.VirtualNetwork(s) — see spec.networks[].network_ref.

relationships.name: disk
relationships.description: Consumes existing/allocated Storage.Volume(s) for its disks.

adopts.role: Vendor-neutral compute-system representation spanning physical AND virtual (Redfish ComputerSystem SystemType: Physical | Virtual | Composed | Partitioned) - the single representative standard for the Compute.* family (shared with Compute.BareMetalHost). Provider-NATIVE realizations (KubeVirt, vSphere, EC2, ...) are declared on the provider support matrix, not on the substrate type.
